updates.tweet_text:
We are now into standard mid-day chop in #ES_F and as always, chop s not to be traded. We trade deep flushes and Failed Breakdowns. The 7427 Failed Breakdown after 930AM was last long trigger. Targets were 7452 (hit), 7462 (hit), 7472 (hit).

7509, then 7523 are next. Magnets https://t.co/gxDzywAh3Q
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7427, 7452, 7462, 7472, 7509, 7523) are either morning-plan levels or reaffirmations/hit targets. No new actionable levels introduced.

updates.tweet_text: @RoyMcAvoyGolf THat is literally the point of the Failed Breakdown. It stops everyone out, then you get in afterwwards
updates.notes: Reply explaining FBD concept to a user; no price levels named, purely educational/conversational content.

updates.tweet_text: @chrisAn00930062 I don't buy any levels, I trade Failed Breakdowns. I don't buy supports ever. When I say a level is weak, it means the level is likely to fail (which it did). Then we can long the Failed Breakdown. That's my whole system
updates.notes: Tweet is a methodology explanation to a user, no actionable price levels named whatsoever.

updates.tweet_text: Its pure mid-day chop now in #ES_F, no trading chop. Now, hold runner for the next magnet which is 7509/7523 https://t.co/4mH1wAaHkQ

updates.tweet_text:

updates.tweet_text: Note: There is nothing to do here but ride runner - no change to newsletter entries. Readers know I do not trade low volatility chop. For me to even open my platform I need a volatility expansion/elevator down sell. If you engage chop, you are trading noise.
updates.notes: Pure trade management/commentary tweet. No prices named at all. Describes a no-trade condition (low volatility chop) with no actionable levels.

updates.tweet_text: @mainftr Yes quite often...usually volatility also dies mid day. Not always though. Most days we get at least one good volatility expansion typically in then morning. Then I make my trade and shut down the platform. Can't trade low volatility
updates.notes: Pure conversational reply about trading habits and volatility patterns. No prices mentioned, no actionable levels named.
